title: File information returns electronically (tax slips and summaries) - Filing with Internet file transfer - Canada.ca
content:
File information returns electronically (tax slips and summaries)

## Filing with Internet file transfer
Internet file transfer allows you to use payroll, commercial, or in-house developed software, to submit an XML file of up to 150 MB over the Internet.
- Have your XML file ready Have your XML file completed and save as .xml. Important considerations: If you are using a commercial software, it will automatically create your .xml file An .xml file created with Web Forms or a PDF document saved as an .xml will not pass validations through Internet file transfer

### Have your XML file ready
Have your XML file completed and save as .xml.
Important considerations:
- If you are using a commercial software, it will automatically create your .xml file
- An .xml file created with Web Forms or a PDF document saved as an .xml will not pass validations through Internet file transfer
- Have your account number and Web access code (WAC) associated to this number To use the Internet file transfer (XML) application, your valid account number and the Web access code (WAC) associated to this number is required. You can also access Internet file transfer (XML) through My Business Account and Represent a Client .

### Have your account number and Web access code (WAC) associated to this number
To use the Internet file transfer (XML) application, your valid account number and the Web access code (WAC) associated to this number is required.
You can also access Internet file transfer (XML) through My Business Account and Represent a Client .
- Log in the Internet file transfer (XML) application You can now browse and load your .xml file in the Internet file transfer (XML) application to send your information return to CRA. Start Internet file transfer (XML)

### Log in the Internet file transfer (XML) application
You can now browse and load your .xml file in the Internet file transfer (XML) application to send your information return to CRA. Start Internet file transfer (XML)
